ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002708 covers ramen noodle soup as a category; no specific torikoku (chicken bone broth) ramen entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW represents chicken-based noodle soup; no torikoku-specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for torikoku ramen or ramen noodle soup at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of torikoku ramen special weighing approximately 550 g, comprising noodles, chicken broth, chicken chashu, soft-boiled egg, bamboo shoots, nori, and green onions.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen, chicken broth-based noodle soup with toppings — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for torikoku ramen exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data: wheat ramen noodles (FDC 168936), chicken broth (FDC 171534), chicken breast cooked (FDC 171477), hard-boiled egg (FDC 173424), bamboo shoots (FDC 170383), nori (FDC 168456), and green onions (FDC 170000). Weighted composite per 100 g of assembled bowl (estimated 550 g total serving). Torikoku-style broth is a rich chicken bone broth (paitan-adjacent) and is non-spicy. Values represent a moderate-richness chicken ramen bowl.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, wheat-based and ramen-type GI values — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for torikoku ramen. GI of 55 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for wheat-based ramen noodles and instant noodle soups (GI range 46–61, mean ~55 for wheat noodle soups). Atkinson et al. 2021 reports GI of wheat noodles at approximately 53–58. A midpoint of 55 (medium GI) is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 x 63.25) / 100 = 34.8, rounded to 34.6 using 11.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g x 550 g serving = 63.25 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from chicken bone broth and soy sauce tare
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit torikoku ramen consumption due to approximately 2640 mg sodium per bowl (480 mg per 100 g x 550 g), which exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg sodium in a single serving; requesting reduced-sodium broth or consuming only half the soup is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index wheat noodles with moderate carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that torikoku ramen has a medium GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 35 per bowl, which can cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise; portion control, choosing a smaller noodle portion, and pairing with the protein-rich toppings (chicken, egg) to blunt glucose response is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from chicken bone broth and chashu
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit torikoku ramen to occasional consumption given its high sodium load (~2640 mg per bowl) and moderate saturated fat (~5 g per bowl), both of which are risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes; opting for a lighter broth and reduced chashu portion is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Torikoku Ramen Special is a Japanese noodle soup dish featuring a rich, opaque chicken bone broth (torikoku, meaning 'chicken bone' in Japanese) that is simmered for many hours to extract collagen and umami from chicken carcasses, resulting in a creamy, milky-white broth similar in style to paitan. The dish is non-spicy and is served with wheat-based ramen noodles, sliced chicken chashu (braised chicken), a soft-boiled marinated egg (ajitsuke tamago), bamboo shoots (menma), sheets of dried nori seaweed, and sliced green onions. The broth is seasoned with a soy sauce tare. The flavour profile is deeply savoury, rich, and umami-forward with a silky mouthfeel from the collagen-rich broth. Torikoku ramen is a lighter alternative to tonkotsu (pork bone) ramen while still delivering a full-bodied soup experience. Nutritionally, a standard bowl provides approximately 539 kcal, 37 g protein, 17.6 g fat, and 63 g carbohydrate per serving. The dish is high in sodium (~2640 mg per bowl) due to the broth and tare, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The medium glycemic index (~55) and moderate glycemic load (~35) require portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is a good source of B vitamins, iron, and protein from the chicken and egg components. This dish is served at Japanese ramen restaurants in Singapore, including Ramen Matsuri.
common_user_queries: What is in chicken bone broth ramen?
How many calories in a bowl of torikoku ramen?
What is the difference between torikoku and tonkotsu ramen?
Is chicken ramen good for diabetics?
How much sodium is in ramen?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for torikoku ramen special exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for wheat ramen noodles, chicken broth, chicken breast, egg, bamboo shoots, nori, and green onions, weighted by estimated proportions in a 550 g bowl. GI is estimated from analogous wheat noodle soup entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component sources. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is a confirmed near-zero value consistent with non-hydrogenated chicken and wheat ingredients. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle soup entries; no direct torikoku ramen GI measurement was found in peer-reviewed literature. Sodium content is notably high due to chicken bone broth and soy sauce tare; clinical caution is warranted for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate for a standard Japanese ramen bowl as served at Ramen Matsuri Singapore. Price SGD 22.15 per bowl as listed on field survey data (Ramen Matsuri, 7 North Canal Road). Halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant; dietary_flags.is_halal is set to false pending verification.
